news.headline: Northgate González Market donates $239,425 to support Latino laborers and families affected by recent wildfires.
news.summary: In response to the destructive wildfires in Eaton Canyon and the Pacific Palisades, Northgate González Market in Anaheim, California offered assistance to the Latino community. This group accounts for almost half of the population in Los Angeles County. The wildfires caused significant damage, prompting the market to provide support to those affected. The Latino community, representing a substantial portion of the county's residents, benefitted from this outreach effort by Northgate González Market.

news.headline: Swaye for Music partners with Trebel to connect with superfans, launching a new platform to enhance artist-fan interactions.
news.summary: Fans of Swaye Artists are now able to engage with artists in deeper ways thanks to Swaye for Music. The platform, set to launch soon, allows superfans to enhance their connection with their favorite artists. This offering aims to provide a more meaningful and interactive experience for fans and artists alike. The company is based in Stamford, Conn. and Phoenix. The launch is scheduled for February 18, 2025.

news.headline: Altcoins like Dogecoin and Cardano may see a breakout as Bitcoin stays range-bound. Trader predicts altseason in 2025 cycle.
news.summary: Bitcoin (BTC) has been range-bound for several weeks, affecting altcoins' momentum. Traders suggest this consolidation phase may pave the way for an altseason breakout. Crypto analyst Astronomer advises patience, noting that major surges in altcoins typically occur in the final cycle year (2025). He compares the current cycle to 2017, where altcoins saw significant growth after a period of stagnation. In 2017, altcoins were flat until the final year, experiencing a surge from March to November. In contrast, 2021 saw an early surge from August 2020 to February 2021, followed by a 70% mid-cycle crash. Bitcoin's current price movement is likened to 2017's gradual rise, differing from 2021's rapid upward momentum. The historical performance of Dogecoin (DOGE) is also discussed in the full story.

news.headline: Bitcoin mining company MARA purchases a Texas wind farm to enhance operations, reusing old rigs in the process.
news.summary: A wind farm located in Texas has been incorporated by MARA into its Bitcoin mining activities. This move has allowed the company to repurpose outdated rigs, enhancing their operational efficiency.

news.headline: Press conference in D.C. on Feb 26 to urge border security funding and immigration reform. Over 10 million consequences discussed.
news.summary: On February 26, 2025, a gathering is scheduled in Washington, D.C., where sheriffs, state legislators, and citizens from various regions will convene to engage with Members of Congress. The focus of the meeting will be the impact of over 10 million instances that have affected their communities.

Edmond's Honor Bourbon by Pronghorn honors Edmond Albius in Madagascar, rooted in purpose. Atlanta, Feb 18, 2025.
news.summary: Edmond's Honor is a unique brand that pays tribute to Edmond Albius, an unknown figure, by aging their product in cognac casks soaked in Madagascar vanilla extract. This brand aims to honor unsung pioneers in various fields like art, culinary arts, and mixology by infusing purpose into its business model. The product was launched on Feb. 18, 2025, in Atlanta. The brand's innovative approach aligns with its commitment to uplifting the next generation of creators and innovators.

news.headline: Dallas College to introduce Bachelor's degree in Software Development in Fall 2025, aiming to offer affordable pathway to high-paying careers.
news.summary: Dallas College has revealed the introduction of a Bachelor of Applied Technology program in Software Development, set to commence in the fall of 2025. This program aims to offer students a cost-effective route towards lucrative careers in the tech industry. The initiative caters to the increasing demand for skilled professionals in this field and seeks to equip students with the necessary skills and knowledge to secure well-paying job opportunities.

news.summary: The value of Solana has sharply decreased due to a reduction in onchain activity, a decline in total value locked (TVL) for decentralized applications (DApps), and the upcoming release of a sizable number of SOL tokens.

news.headline: Global coffee machine market estimated to reach $15.1 billion by 2029, with a CAGR of 3.5%, driven by smart tech and sustainability.
news.summary: Cutting-edge advancements in smart technology and a growing focus on sustainability are driving significant innovation in the worldwide coffee machine industry. A recent report by BCC Research projects that the "Global Market for Coffee Machines" will soar to an estimated value of $15.1 billion by 2029, experiencing a steady Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 3.5%. This indicates a promising trajectory for the sector as it capitalizes on evolving consumer preferences and technological capabilities.

news.headline: HitPaw to integrate DeepSeek AI into FotorPea for enhanced image generation, promising a more powerful AI experience.

This integration will bring a significant boost to the functionality and advanced capabilities of FotorPea by leveraging the power of DeepSeek AI. Users can anticipate a more robust and sophisticated AI-driven image generation process, providing them with improved results and a more seamless experience when creating visuals. This update is scheduled to roll out soon, marking a significant advancement in HitPaw's technology offerings.

This decision is fueled by the growing interest in crypto assets and blockchain technology. The Pyth Trust joins Grayscale's portfolio of investment products, indicating the company's commitment to catering to the evolving needs of investors. This move reflects the rising trend of institutional investors seeking exposure to digital assets, with Grayscale visionary in capitalizing on this demand. It is a strategic addition as the crypto market continues to gain traction, highlighting Grayscale's proactive approach in the rapidly developing crypto investment landscape.

news.headline: U.S. Chamber report shows lawsuits cost Texas families almost $5,000 yearly. Lone Star Alliance urges reforms in courts.
news.summary: A recent study conducted by the U.S. Chamber of Commerce revealed that lawsuits are causing Texas families an average financial burden of approximately $5,000 annually. This information prompted the Lone Star Economic Alliance to urge the state legislature in Austin to take action in reinstating common sense, transparency, and fairness within the court system. The report's findings highlight the urgent need for reforms that prioritize the well-being and financial stability of families in Texas.

news.headline: Nasdaq-100 hits record high at 22,191.37, Meta stock surges, closing higher 20 days in a row, driving market momentum.
news.summary: The Nasdaq-100 reached a record high of 22,191.37, with tech stocks leading the surge. The Invesco QQQ Trust (NASDAQ:QQQ) hit a new 52-week high of $540. Meta Platforms Inc (NASDAQ:META) closed higher for 20 consecutive trading days and has increased by 16% since Jan. 16, contributing significantly to the Nasdaq's rise. Despite a 3.8% drop on Tuesday, Meta's strong performance has driven the tech-heavy Nasdaq and the overall market upwards. Mark Zuckerberg’s Meta Stock story can be found on Benzinga.com.

While Nvidia witnessed a rally, Meta faced a drop. The stock market update indicates that the Dow Jones slipped, with significant movements seen in various key stocks. Warren Buffett showed interest in particular stocks, contributing to market fluctuations. Companies like Nvidia witnessed positive momentum, whereas Meta encountered a decline. This dynamic shift in the market reflects the impact of recent events and key players' decisions.

The Series B preferred shares (CUSIP #83600C301), Series C preferred shares (CUSIP #83600C400), and Series D preferred shares (CUSIP #83600C509) are included in this announcement. The income tax classification for the 2024 distributions, to be reported on Form 1099-DIV, is detailed in the provided table. news.headline: Graphite industry report for 2019-2034, including manufacturing, capacity, production, consumption, trade, and price analysis.
news.summary: A report titled "Graphite World, Regions and Countries Market Review 2019-2024 and Forecast to 2034" has recently been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. This report serves as a crucial source of information regarding the global graphite market. It presents comprehensive data on both global and regional markets, as well as specific countries, offering analysis from 2019 to 2024 along with forecasts extending to 2034 concerning supply, demand, prices, and downstream industries. The report contains a wealth of high-quality tables and figures.

news.headline: Analyst predicts revenue opportunities for CoreCivic in 2026, with Q4 revenue at $479.3M and FFO of 39 cents per share.
news.summary: Research analyst Jason Weaver from JonesResearch has reaffirmed a Buy rating and a price target of $28 for CoreCivic, Inc. (NYSE:CXW) post the release of its fourth-quarter results. The company reported revenue of $479.3 million, surpassing analysts' predictions of $465.17 million, along with funds from operations (FFO) of 39 cents per share, which also exceeded analyst estimates of 34 cents per share. CoreCivic anticipates its full-year 2025 net income to fall within the range of $53.5 million to $67.5 million and full-year FFO between $1.37 to $1.50 per share. For further details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Northern Oil and Gas reports 131-132 MBoe daily production for Q4 2024, with $27M acquisitions and $231-$232M capex. CEO optimistic for future despite setbacks. 2025 production forecasted at 130-135 MBoe per day.
news.summary: Northern Oil and Gas, Inc. (NYSE:NOG) shared their operational update for the fourth quarter of 2024 and outlook for 2025. They anticipate a production rate of 131.0–132.0 thousand barrels of oil equivalent (MBoe) per day in Q4, which aligns with their yearly guidance. The company invested $27 million in Ground Game and leasehold acquisitions during the quarter. Additionally, capital expenditures for Q4 are estimated to range between $231 million and $232 million, excluding non-budgeted transactions. Despite challenges in late 2024 and early 2025, including a production slowdown, NOG's CEO Nick O’Grady highlighted a 25% production growth throughout 2024. Looking ahead, the company predicts a production range of 130.0–135.0 MBoe per day for 2025.

news.headline: Asbury Automotive buys Herb Chambers for $1.34 billion, acquiring 33 dealerships and 52 franchises, targeting $2.9 billion revenue in 2024.
news.summary: Shares of Asbury Automotive Group Inc (NYSE:ABG) are experiencing a decline today after the company announced a significant acquisition. As part of a $1.34 billion agreement, Asbury will be acquiring The Herb Chambers Companies. This deal includes 33 dealerships, 52 franchises, and three collision centers operated by Herb Chambers, which collectively generated $2.9 billion in revenue in 2024. The transaction is set to be finalized in the latter part of the second quarter of 2025, pending standard regulatory approvals. Funding for the acquisition will come from a combination of credit facilities, mortgage proceeds, and cash reserves. Herb Chambers, the owner of The Herb Chambers Companies, ... (Full details can be found on Benzinga.com)
